title: A better bitset for enum flags – Why is a raven like a writing desk?
excerpt: TL;DR: using enum for bitflags is poor, but it’s what we’re likely to end up with.
content_html:
TL;DR: using enum for bitflags is poor, but it’s what we’re likely to end up with.

Many electrons have been spent expounding enumeration bitflag types (and not just in C++). As a tech community across multiple languages, we seem to be all in on the idea that the right way for this to look is something like:

// I somehow opt in for bitwise operations on this type
enum struct permissions {
  NONE  = 0,
  READ  = 1 << 0,
  WRITE = 1 << 1,
  EXEC  = 1 << 2
};

// then, I can use "regular bitwise operators"
auto p = permissions::READ | permissions::WRITE;
if ((p & permissions::READ) == permissions::READ) {
  // ...
}

Where in C++, opting in variously looks like adding a function declaration found by ADL, or adding a reflection annotation, or using a macro to provide operators, or…

We might well end up here in C++. With successive standards, we get new techniques which enable us to get there a little more easily, and C++26 reflection is the latest example of that. P4313 is a proposal for enum flags in this style.

I think it’s a workable approach (i.e. we will make it work), but ultimately it seems to me that it’s the wrong direction. I think using a bitset is a much better solution. Unfortunately, std::bitset can’t get us there, which is why we may end up with the enum bitflags.

The pros of enum bitflags are usually given as:

  1. Type safety (at least when using scoped enumerations). There is no implicit conversion between values of different types. We can’t accidentally confuse permissions with some other value.
  2. Ergonomics of use. Using these things looks like using plain old integers. (I note that P4313 says “plain C-style int“, notwithstanding clang-tidy’s bugprone-signed-bitwise check.)

I think the downsides are:

  1. Ergonomics of implementation. As I said, problems are lessened with each C++ standard. And this is something the culture of C++ library authorship discounts: who cares how difficult it is to implement under the hood? But it’s still going to be kind of annoying to apply bit operations to enumeration types you don’t own.
  2. Aesthetics. I don’t want using these things to look like using plain old integers, because using plain old integers is fraught with peril. Which leads to…
  3. Ergonomics of use. Bitwise operators famously have the “wrong” precedence in C and C++, for historical reasons. Yes, please use your compiler warnings. Ironically the expositional code used in P4313 exhibits this well-known operator precedence error (which I corrected above)!
  4. (Perhaps most significantly…) Conflation of ideas. enum bitflags are easy, but not simple. They complect naming bits (what we actually want) with representing them. True, it’s not our fault; this is the legacy of C that multiple languages double down on at this point. But it’s also not right. We say “the type has members A and B” then we pretend that A|B is also a member of the type. But that’s immediately subverting type safety: we have confused representation with naming.

So what’s the alternative? Well unfortunately, std::bitset doesn’t cut it, and in all probability can’t be made to cut it. But a better bitset could, and is totally implementable. Here’s what I’d like it to look like.

enum struct permissions {
  READ, WRITE, EXEC,
  MAX // here's the conventional opt-in
};

// bitset can take an enum, and preserve it (type safety)
using perms_t = bitset<permissions::MAX>;

// how about a tag constructor?
auto p = perms_t{place_bits, permissions::READ, permissions::WRITE};
if (p[permissions::READ]) {
  // ...
}

As far as I can see, this solves almost (I’m being conservative in saying almost) all the problems with the enumeration approach.

  1. Type safety. I can’t assign a perms_t here from some other bitset or from an integral type. And this is true even for unscoped enums I find in 3rd party libraries.
  2. Ergonomics of use. operator[] isn’t going to have the precedence problems of the bitwise operators. Enumeration values don’t have to be pre-shifted, avoiding a source of potential error.
  3. Correct separation of concerns. The enumeration type names the bits. The bitset represents them. We aren’t pretending that READ|WRITE is a member of permissions.

It still ticks the other boxes too. Implementation is at least as easy as, if not easier than, the enum approach.

I think the one remaining problem is around the ergonomics of taking a 3rd party codebase whose enum(s) I don’t own, and treating the enums as bits. Fair criticism. I think we are always going to find that there are ways this can be annoying. With the enum bitflags approach I would use an intrusive opt-in mechanism something like injecting ADL-found functions into a 3rd party namespace. With a bitset approach, it’s not intrusive, but I still need to provide a “max” value (or otherwise specify the number of bits in some other API shape variation not shown here).

Both of these techniques mean that I might have to change code when upstream code changes. I think on balance the bitset approach is a bit easier, especially considering that we can probably use reflection to discover a suitable “max” value. (Unless a 3rd party library deliberately hides the enum values away, and in that case, what would we use to name the bits anyway?)

But you’re still thinking this isn’t as nice as it could be, and you’re right, because so far you’re imagining std::bitset with just these extras. For nice usage, this bitset needs a few more things.

If we’re using a bitset for representation, well actually we want an easy way to specify and get access to the underlying thing (like enum can deal with its underlying type). Because ultimately types are all just compile-time fluff that the runtime doesn’t care about: these things do need to be plain integral stuff under the hood.

So something like this:

using B1 = bitset<8>;                // 8 bits, a std::uint8_t underneath
using B2 = bitset<8, std::uint32_t>; // 8 bits, a std::uint32_t
using B3 = bitset<64>;               // 64 bits, a std::uint64_t
using B4 = bitset<64, std::uint8_t>; // 64 bits, a std::array<std::uint8_t, 8>

enum struct permissions { READ, WRITE, EXEC, MAX };
using B = bitset<permissions::MAX, std::uint32_t>;

Because we want to be able to make these size/speed tradeoffs appropriately across platforms from tiny microcontrollers to massive servers.

And because this thing is just integral stuff that we want to write to some serialization format, we want an easy way to do that.

auto perms = get_permissions();                // a bitset over permissions
auto serial_perms = perms.to<std::uint16_t>(); // a std::uint16_t for serialization

We can of course add several other nice things to a bitset type, for other uses cases. (e.g. Why is it so hard, in a generic way, to construct a std::bitset with all the bits set? How do I iterate over set bits? How do I find the lowest unset bit?) But all that’s a different story.

Well, that’s about it. I think this bitset approach is much better than the enum flags approach. It’s better even after successive C++ versions iron out more and more of the annoying niceties of enum flags implementation. But we’re going to end up with enum flags, aren’t we? And I guess we’ll just make it work.

blocks.html_en: TL;DR: using enum for bitflags is poor, but it’s what we’re likely to end up with.

sentences.en: TL;DR: using enum for bitflags is poor, but it’s what we’re likely to end up with.
sentences.zh: TL;DR：使用枚举作为位标志很糟糕，但这就是我们最终可能得到的结果。

sentences.en: Many electrons have been spent expounding enumeration bitflag types (and not just in C++).
sentences.zh: 许多电子都花在了阐述枚举位标志类型上（而不仅仅是在 C++ 中）。

sentences.en: As a tech community across multiple languages, we seem to be all in on the idea that the right way for this to look is something like:
sentences.zh: 作为跨多种语言的技术社区，我们似乎都认为正确的方式是这样的：

blocks.html_en: Where in C++, opting in variously looks like adding a function declaration found by ADL, or adding a reflection annotation, or using a macro to provide operators, or…

sentences.en: Where in C++, opting in variously looks like adding a function declaration found by ADL, or adding a reflection annotation, or using a macro to provide operators, or…
sentences.zh: 在 C++ 中，选择加入的方式多种多样，比如添加 ADL 找到的函数声明，或者添加反射注释，或者使用宏来提供运算符，或者……

blocks.html_en: We might well end up here in C++. With successive standards, we get new techniques which enable us to get there a little more easily, and C++26 reflection is the latest example of that. P4313 is a proposal for enum flags in this style.

sentences.en: We might well end up here in C++.
sentences.zh: 我们很可能最终会使用 C++。

sentences.en: With successive standards, we get new techniques which enable us to get there a little more easily, and C++26 reflection is the latest example of that.
sentences.zh: 通过相继的标准，我们获得了新技术，使我们能够更轻松地实现这一目标，C++26 反射就是最新的例子。

sentences.en: P4313 is a proposal for enum flags in this style.
sentences.zh: P4313 是这种风格的枚举标志的提案。

blocks.html_en: I think it’s a workable approach (i.e. we will make it work), but ultimately it seems to me that it’s the wrong direction. I think using a bitset is a much better solution. Unfortunately, std::bitset can’t get us there, which is why we may end up with the enum bitflags.

sentences.en: I think it’s a workable approach (i.e. we will make it work), but ultimately it seems to me that it’s the wrong direction.
sentences.zh: 我认为这是一种可行的方法（即我们将使其发挥作用），但最终在我看来这是错误的方向。

sentences.en: I think using a bitset is a much better solution.
sentences.zh: 我认为使用位集是一个更好的解决方案。

sentences.en: Unfortunately, std::bitset can’t get us there, which is why we may end up with the enum bitflags.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，std::bitset 无法让我们到达那里，这就是为什么我们最终可能会得到枚举位标志。

blocks.html_en: The pros of enum bitflags are usually given as:

sentences.en: The pros of enum bitflags are usually given as:
sentences.zh: 枚举位标志的优点通常如下：

blocks.html_en: • Type safety (at least when using scoped enumerations). There is no implicit conversion between values of different types. We can’t accidentally confuse permissions with some other value.

sentences.en: Type safety (at least when using scoped enumerations).
sentences.zh: 类型安全（至少在使用作用域枚举时）。

sentences.en: There is no implicit conversion between values of different types.
sentences.zh: 不同类型的值之间不存在隐式转换。

sentences.en: We can’t accidentally confuse permissions with some other value.
sentences.zh: 我们不能意外地将权限与其他值混淆。

blocks.html_en: • Ergonomics of use. Using these things looks like using plain old integers. (I note that P4313 says “plain C-style int“, notwithstanding clang-tidy’s bugprone-signed-bitwise check.)

sentences.en: Ergonomics of use.
sentences.zh: 使用人体工程学。

sentences.en: Using these things looks like using plain old integers.
sentences.zh: 使用这些东西看起来就像使用普通的旧整数。

sentences.en: (I note that P4313 says “plain C-style int“, notwithstanding clang-tidy’s bugprone-signed-bitwise check.)
sentences.zh: （我注意到 P4313 说的是“plain C-style int”，尽管 clang-tidy 进行了容易出错的按位签名检查。）

sentences.en: I think the downsides are:
sentences.zh: 我认为缺点是：

blocks.html_en: • Ergonomics of implementation. As I said, problems are lessened with each C++ standard. And this is something the culture of C++ library authorship discounts: who cares how difficult it is to implement under the hood? But it’s still going to be kind of annoying to apply bit operations to enumeration types you don’t own.

sentences.en: Ergonomics of implementation.
sentences.zh: 实施的人体工程学。

sentences.en: As I said, problems are lessened with each C++ standard.
sentences.zh: 正如我所说，每个 C++ 标准都会减少问题。

sentences.en: And this is something the culture of C++ library authorship discounts: who cares how difficult it is to implement under the hood?
sentences.zh: 这是 C++ 库作者文化所忽视的事情：谁在乎它在幕后实现起来有多困难？

sentences.en: But it’s still going to be kind of annoying to apply bit operations to enumeration types you don’t own.
sentences.zh: 但将位运算应用于您不拥有的枚举类型仍然会有点烦人。

blocks.html_en: • Aesthetics. I don’t want using these things to look like using plain old integers, because using plain old integers is fraught with peril. Which leads to…

sentences.en: Aesthetics.
sentences.zh: 美学。

sentences.en: I don’t want using these things to look like using plain old integers, because using plain old integers is fraught with peril.
sentences.zh: 我不希望使用这些东西看起来像使用普通的旧整数，因为使用普通的旧整数充满了危险。

sentences.en: Which leads to…
sentences.zh: 这导致……

sentences.en: Ergonomics of use.
sentences.zh: 使用人体工程学。

sentences.en: Bitwise operators famously have the “wrong” precedence in C and C++, for historical reasons.
sentences.zh: 由于历史原因，位运算符在 C 和 C++ 中具有“错误”的优先级。

sentences.en: Yes, please use your compiler warnings.
sentences.zh: 是的，请使用编译器警告。

sentences.en: Ironically the expositional code used in P4313 exhibits this well-known operator precedence error (which I corrected above)!
sentences.zh: 讽刺的是，P4313 中使用的说明性代码表现出了这个众所周知的运算符优先级错误（我在上面更正了该错误）！

blocks.html_en: • (Perhaps most significantly…) Conflation of ideas. enum bitflags are easy, but not simple. They complect naming bits (what we actually want) with representing them. True, it’s not our fault; this is the legacy of C that multiple languages double down on at this point. But it’s also not right. We say “the type has members A and B” then we pretend that A|B is also a member of the type. But that’s immediately subverting type safety: we have confused representation with naming.

sentences.en: (Perhaps most significantly…)
sentences.zh: （也许最重要的是……）

sentences.en: Conflation of ideas.
sentences.zh: 想法的合并。

sentences.en: enum bitflags are easy, but not simple.
sentences.zh: 枚举位标志很容易，但并不简单。

sentences.en: They complect naming bits (what we actually want) with representing them.
sentences.zh: 它们使命名位（我们真正想要的）与表示它们变得复杂。

sentences.en: True, it’s not our fault; this is the legacy of C that multiple languages double down on at this point.
sentences.zh: 确实，这不是我们的错；这是我们的错。这是 C 的遗产，多种语言在这一点上加倍努力。

sentences.en: But it’s also not right.
sentences.zh: 但这也是不对的。

sentences.en: We say “the type has members A and B” then we pretend that A|B is also a member of the type.
sentences.zh: 我们说“该类型有成员 A 和 B”，然后我们假装 A|B 也是该类型的成员。

sentences.en: But that’s immediately subverting type safety: we have confused representation with naming.
sentences.zh: 但这立即破坏了类型安全：我们混淆了表示和命名。

blocks.html_en: So what’s the alternative? Well unfortunately, std::bitset doesn’t cut it, and in all probability can’t be made to cut it. But a better bitset could, and is totally implementable. Here’s what I’d like it to look like.

sentences.en: So what’s the alternative?
sentences.zh: 那么还有什么选择呢？

sentences.en: Well unfortunately, std::bitset doesn’t cut it, and in all probability can’t be made to cut it.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是， std::bitset 不会删除它，并且很可能无法删除它。

sentences.en: But a better bitset could, and is totally implementable.
sentences.zh: 但更好的位集可以而且完全可以实现。

sentences.en: Here’s what I’d like it to look like.
sentences.zh: 这就是我想要的样子。

blocks.html_en: As far as I can see, this solves almost (I’m being conservative in saying almost) all the problems with the enumeration approach.

sentences.en: As far as I can see, this solves almost (I’m being conservative in saying almost) all the problems with the enumeration approach.
sentences.zh: 据我所知，这几乎解决了（我保守地说几乎）枚举方法的所有问题。

blocks.html_en: • Type safety. I can’t assign a perms_t here from some other bitset or from an integral type. And this is true even for unscoped enums I find in 3rd party libraries.

sentences.en: Type safety.
sentences.zh: 类型安全。

sentences.en: I can’t assign a perms_t here from some other bitset or from an integral type.
sentences.zh: 我无法在此处从其他位集或整数类型分配 perms_t。

sentences.en: And this is true even for unscoped enums I find in 3rd party libraries.
sentences.zh: 即使对于我在第三方库中找到的无范围枚举也是如此。

blocks.html_en: • Ergonomics of use. operator[] isn’t going to have the precedence problems of the bitwise operators. Enumeration values don’t have to be pre-shifted, avoiding a source of potential error.

sentences.en: Ergonomics of use.
sentences.zh: 使用人体工程学。

sentences.en: operator[] isn’t going to have the precedence problems of the bitwise operators.
sentences.zh: operator[] 不会出现按位运算符的优先级问题。

sentences.en: Enumeration values don’t have to be pre-shifted, avoiding a source of potential error.
sentences.zh: 枚举值不必预先移动，从而避免潜在错误的来源。

blocks.html_en: • Correct separation of concerns. The enumeration type names the bits. The bitset represents them. We aren’t pretending that READ|WRITE is a member of permissions.

sentences.en: Correct separation of concerns.
sentences.zh: 正确的关注点分离。

sentences.en: The enumeration type names the bits.
sentences.zh: 枚举类型对位进行命名。

sentences.en: The bitset represents them.
sentences.zh: 位集代表它们。

sentences.en: We aren’t pretending that READ|WRITE is a member of permissions.
sentences.zh: 我们并不是假装 READ|WRITE 是权限的成员。

blocks.html_en: It still ticks the other boxes too. Implementation is at least as easy as, if not easier than, the enum approach.

sentences.en: It still ticks the other boxes too.
sentences.zh: 它仍然勾选了其他框。

sentences.en: Implementation is at least as easy as, if not easier than, the enum approach.
sentences.zh: 实现至少与枚举方法一样简单，甚至更容易。

blocks.html_en: I think the one remaining problem is around the ergonomics of taking a 3rd party codebase whose enum(s) I don’t own, and treating the enums as bits. Fair criticism. I think we are always going to find that there are ways this can be annoying. With the enum bitflags approach I would use an intrusive opt-in mechanism something like injecting ADL-found functions into a 3rd party namespace. With a bitset approach, it’s not intrusive, but I still need to provide a “max” value (or otherwise specify the number of bits in some other API shape variation not shown here).

sentences.en: I think the one remaining problem is around the ergonomics of taking a 3rd party codebase whose enum(s) I don’t own, and treating the enums as bits.
sentences.zh: 我认为剩下的一个问题是关于采用我不拥有的枚举的第三方代码库并将枚举视为位的人体工程学问题。

sentences.en: Fair criticism.
sentences.zh: 公平的批评。

sentences.en: I think we are always going to find that there are ways this can be annoying.
sentences.zh: 我认为我们总是会发现这在某些方面可能很烦人。

sentences.en: With the enum bitflags approach I would use an intrusive opt-in mechanism something like injecting ADL-found functions into a 3rd party namespace.
sentences.zh: 通过枚举位标志方法，我将使用侵入式选择加入机制，例如将 ADL 发现的函数注入到第 3 方命名空间中。

sentences.en: With a bitset approach, it’s not intrusive, but I still need to provide a “max” value (or otherwise specify the number of bits in some other API shape variation not shown here).
sentences.zh: 使用位集方法，它不是侵入性的，但我仍然需要提供一个“最大值”（或者以其他方式指定此处未显示的其他 API 形状变体中的位数）。

blocks.html_en: Both of these techniques mean that I might have to change code when upstream code changes. I think on balance the bitset approach is a bit easier, especially considering that we can probably use reflection to discover a suitable “max” value. (Unless a 3rd party library deliberately hides the enum values away, and in that case, what would we use to name the bits anyway?)

sentences.en: Both of these techniques mean that I might have to change code when upstream code changes.
sentences.zh: 这两种技术意味着当上游代码更改时我可能必须更改代码。

sentences.en: I think on balance the bitset approach is a bit easier, especially considering that we can probably use reflection to discover a suitable “max” value.
sentences.zh: 我认为总的来说，位集方法更容易一些，特别是考虑到我们可以使用反射来发现合适的“最大值”。

sentences.en: (Unless a 3rd party library deliberately hides the enum values away, and in that case, what would we use to name the bits anyway?)
sentences.zh: （除非第三方库故意隐藏枚举值，在这种情况下，我们将使用什么来命名这些位？）

blocks.html_en: But you’re still thinking this isn’t as nice as it could be, and you’re right, because so far you’re imagining std::bitset with just these extras. For nice usage, this bitset needs a few more things.

sentences.en: But you’re still thinking this isn’t as nice as it could be, and you’re right, because so far you’re imagining std::bitset with just these extras.
sentences.zh: 但你仍然认为这并没有想象的那么好，你是对的，因为到目前为止你想象的 std::bitset 就只有这些额外的东西。

sentences.en: For nice usage, this bitset needs a few more things.
sentences.zh: 为了更好地使用，这个位集还需要一些东西。

blocks.html_en: If we’re using a bitset for representation, well actually we want an easy way to specify and get access to the underlying thing (like enum can deal with its underlying type). Because ultimately types are all just compile-time fluff that the runtime doesn’t care about: these things do need to be plain integral stuff under the hood.

sentences.en: If we’re using a bitset for representation, well actually we want an easy way to specify and get access to the underlying thing (like enum can deal with its underlying type).
sentences.zh: 如果我们使用位集进行表示，那么实际上我们需要一种简单的方法来指定和访问底层事物（例如枚举可以处理其底层类型）。

sentences.en: Because ultimately types are all just compile-time fluff that the runtime doesn’t care about: these things do need to be plain integral stuff under the hood.
sentences.zh: 因为最终类型都只是运行时不关心的编译时绒毛：这些东西确实需要在幕后是简单的、完整的东西。

blocks.html_en: So something like this:

sentences.en: So something like this:
sentences.zh: 所以像这样：

blocks.html_en: Because we want to be able to make these size/speed tradeoffs appropriately across platforms from tiny microcontrollers to massive servers.

sentences.en: Because we want to be able to make these size/speed tradeoffs appropriately across platforms from tiny microcontrollers to massive servers.
sentences.zh: 因为我们希望能够在从微型微控制器到大型服务器的平台上适当地进行这些尺寸/速度的权衡。

blocks.html_en: And because this thing is just integral stuff that we want to write to some serialization format, we want an easy way to do that.

sentences.en: And because this thing is just integral stuff that we want to write to some serialization format, we want an easy way to do that.
sentences.zh: 因为这个东西只是我们想要写入某种序列化格式的完整内容，所以我们需要一种简单的方法来做到这一点。

blocks.html_en: We can of course add several other nice things to a bitset type, for other uses cases. (e.g. Why is it so hard, in a generic way, to construct a std::bitset with all the bits set? How do I iterate over set bits? How do I find the lowest unset bit?) But all that’s a different story.

sentences.en: We can of course add several other nice things to a bitset type, for other uses cases.
sentences.zh: 当然，我们可以为位集类型添加其他一些好东西，以用于其他用例。

sentences.en: (e.g. Why is it so hard, in a generic way, to construct a std::bitset with all the bits set?
sentences.zh: （例如，为什么以通用的方式构造一个包含所有位集的 std::bitset 如此困难？

sentences.en: How do I iterate over set bits?
sentences.zh: 如何迭代设置的位？

sentences.en: How do I find the lowest unset bit?)
sentences.zh: 如何找到最低的未设置位？）

sentences.en: But all that’s a different story.
sentences.zh: 但所有这些都是不同的故事。

blocks.html_en: Well, that’s about it. I think this bitset approach is much better than the enum flags approach. It’s better even after successive C++ versions iron out more and more of the annoying niceties of enum flags implementation. But we’re going to end up with enum flags, aren’t we? And I guess we’ll just make it work.

sentences.en: Well, that’s about it.
sentences.zh: 嗯，就是这样。

sentences.en: I think this bitset approach is much better than the enum flags approach.
sentences.zh: 我认为这种位集方法比枚举标志方法要好得多。

sentences.en: It’s better even after successive C++ versions iron out more and more of the annoying niceties of enum flags implementation.
sentences.zh: 即使在连续的 C++ 版本消除了枚举标志实现中越来越多烦人的细节之后，它仍然更好。

sentences.en: But we’re going to end up with enum flags, aren’t we?
sentences.zh: 但我们最终会得到枚举标志，不是吗？

sentences.en: And I guess we’ll just make it work.
sentences.zh: 我想我们会让它发挥作用。

content_html_zh:
TL;DR：使用枚举作为位标志很糟糕，但这就是我们最终可能得到的结果。

许多电子都花在了阐述枚举位标志类型上（而不仅仅是在 C++ 中）。 作为跨多种语言的技术社区，我们似乎都认为正确的方式是这样的：

// I somehow opt in for bitwise operations on this type
enum struct permissions {
  NONE  = 0,
  READ  = 1 << 0,
  WRITE = 1 << 1,
  EXEC  = 1 << 2
};

// then, I can use "regular bitwise operators"
auto p = permissions::READ | permissions::WRITE;
if ((p & permissions::READ) == permissions::READ) {
  // ...
}

在 C++ 中，选择加入的方式多种多样，比如添加 ADL 找到的函数声明，或者添加反射注释，或者使用宏来提供运算符，或者……

我们很可能最终会使用 C++。 通过相继的标准，我们获得了新技术，使我们能够更轻松地实现这一目标，C++26 反射就是最新的例子。 P4313 是这种风格的枚举标志的提案。

我认为这是一种可行的方法（即我们将使其发挥作用），但最终在我看来这是错误的方向。 我认为使用位集是一个更好的解决方案。 不幸的是，std::bitset 无法让我们到达那里，这就是为什么我们最终可能会得到枚举位标志。

枚举位标志的优点通常如下：

  1. 类型安全（至少在使用作用域枚举时）。 不同类型的值之间不存在隐式转换。 我们不能意外地将权限与其他值混淆。
  2. 使用人体工程学。 使用这些东西看起来就像使用普通的旧整数。 （我注意到 P4313 说的是“plain C-style int”，尽管 clang-tidy 进行了容易出错的按位签名检查。）

我认为缺点是：

  1. 实施的人体工程学。 正如我所说，每个 C++ 标准都会减少问题。 这是 C++ 库作者文化所忽视的事情：谁在乎它在幕后实现起来有多困难？ 但将位运算应用于您不拥有的枚举类型仍然会有点烦人。
  2. 美学。 我不希望使用这些东西看起来像使用普通的旧整数，因为使用普通的旧整数充满了危险。 这导致……
  3. 使用人体工程学。 由于历史原因，位运算符在 C 和 C++ 中具有“错误”的优先级。 是的，请使用编译器警告。 讽刺的是，P4313 中使用的说明性代码表现出了这个众所周知的运算符优先级错误（我在上面更正了该错误）！
  4. （也许最重要的是……） 想法的合并。 枚举位标志很容易，但并不简单。 它们使命名位（我们真正想要的）与表示它们变得复杂。 确实，这不是我们的错；这是我们的错。这是 C 的遗产，多种语言在这一点上加倍努力。 但这也是不对的。 我们说“该类型有成员 A 和 B”，然后我们假装 A|B 也是该类型的成员。 但这立即破坏了类型安全：我们混淆了表示和命名。

那么还有什么选择呢？ 不幸的是， std::bitset 不会删除它，并且很可能无法删除它。 但更好的位集可以而且完全可以实现。 这就是我想要的样子。

enum struct permissions {
  READ, WRITE, EXEC,
  MAX // here's the conventional opt-in
};

// bitset can take an enum, and preserve it (type safety)
using perms_t = bitset<permissions::MAX>;

// how about a tag constructor?
auto p = perms_t{place_bits, permissions::READ, permissions::WRITE};
if (p[permissions::READ]) {
  // ...
}

据我所知，这几乎解决了（我保守地说几乎）枚举方法的所有问题。

  1. 类型安全。 我无法在此处从其他位集或整数类型分配 perms_t。 即使对于我在第三方库中找到的无范围枚举也是如此。
  2. 使用人体工程学。 operator[] 不会出现按位运算符的优先级问题。 枚举值不必预先移动，从而避免潜在错误的来源。
  3. 正确的关注点分离。 枚举类型对位进行命名。 位集代表它们。 我们并不是假装 READ|WRITE 是权限的成员。

它仍然勾选了其他框。 实现至少与枚举方法一样简单，甚至更容易。

我认为剩下的一个问题是关于采用我不拥有的枚举的第三方代码库并将枚举视为位的人体工程学问题。 公平的批评。 我认为我们总是会发现这在某些方面可能很烦人。 通过枚举位标志方法，我将使用侵入式选择加入机制，例如将 ADL 发现的函数注入到第 3 方命名空间中。 使用位集方法，它不是侵入性的，但我仍然需要提供一个“最大值”（或者以其他方式指定此处未显示的其他 API 形状变体中的位数）。

这两种技术意味着当上游代码更改时我可能必须更改代码。 我认为总的来说，位集方法更容易一些，特别是考虑到我们可以使用反射来发现合适的“最大值”。 （除非第三方库故意隐藏枚举值，在这种情况下，我们将使用什么来命名这些位？）

但你仍然认为这并没有想象的那么好，你是对的，因为到目前为止你想象的 std::bitset 就只有这些额外的东西。 为了更好地使用，这个位集还需要一些东西。

如果我们使用位集进行表示，那么实际上我们需要一种简单的方法来指定和访问底层事物（例如枚举可以处理其底层类型）。 因为最终类型都只是运行时不关心的编译时绒毛：这些东西确实需要在幕后是简单的、完整的东西。

所以像这样：

using B1 = bitset<8>;                // 8 bits, a std::uint8_t underneath
using B2 = bitset<8, std::uint32_t>; // 8 bits, a std::uint32_t
using B3 = bitset<64>;               // 64 bits, a std::uint64_t
using B4 = bitset<64, std::uint8_t>; // 64 bits, a std::array<std::uint8_t, 8>

enum struct permissions { READ, WRITE, EXEC, MAX };
using B = bitset<permissions::MAX, std::uint32_t>;

因为我们希望能够在从微型微控制器到大型服务器的平台上适当地进行这些尺寸/速度的权衡。

因为这个东西只是我们想要写入某种序列化格式的完整内容，所以我们需要一种简单的方法来做到这一点。

auto perms = get_permissions();                // a bitset over permissions
auto serial_perms = perms.to<std::uint16_t>(); // a std::uint16_t for serialization

当然，我们可以为位集类型添加其他一些好东西，以用于其他用例。 （例如，为什么以通用的方式构造一个包含所有位集的 std::bitset 如此困难？ 如何迭代设置的位？ 如何找到最低的未设置位？） 但所有这些都是不同的故事。

嗯，就是这样。 我认为这种位集方法比枚举标志方法要好得多。 即使在连续的 C++ 版本消除了枚举标志实现中越来越多烦人的细节之后，它仍然更好。 但我们最终会得到枚举标志，不是吗？ 我想我们会让它发挥作用。
